# Forebear Find — Italian Ancestry Research & Genealogy Services
> Professional Italian genealogy research for dual citizenship, record retrieval, and family history. Based in Vernal, Utah with direct access to archives across all 20 Italian regions.

## About
Forebear Find is a professional genealogy research firm led by Rocco DeLuca, specializing in Italian ancestry research for dual citizenship applications (jure sanguinis), vital record retrieval, and narrative family history. We work directly with Italian civil archives, parish records, state archives, and military conscription lists across all 20 regions of Italy, with particular depth in Abruzzo, Calabria, Campania, and Sicily. ICAPGen accreditation in progress.

## Contact
- Website: https://forebearfind.com
- Email: info@forebearfind.com
- Phone: +1 (435) 219-5120
- Location: Vernal, Utah 84078

## Core Services
- Italian Dual Citizenship by Descent (Jure Sanguinis): Complete lineage documentation, vital record retrieval from Italian comuni, naturalization verification, and application preparation.
- Italian Birth Record Search: Retrieval of original birth certificates (atto di nascita) from Italian civil and parish archives.
- Italian Marriage Record Search: Retrieval of marriage certificates (atto di matrimonio) and processetti matrimoniali.
- Missing Record Research: Alternative documentation strategies when civil records are destroyed or missing — parish records, military conscription lists (liste di leva), emigration registers, state archive duplicates.
- Destroyed Record Recovery: Systematic archival research to recover Italian vital records lost to WWII bombing, earthquakes, floods, and municipal neglect using Italy's dual-copy system and alternative sources.
- Name Discrepancy Resolution: Documenting identity across Americanized names, dialect variations, and clerical errors.
- 1948 Matrilineal Line Cases: Genealogical documentation for judicial petitions involving female ancestors who had children before January 1, 1948.
- Proving Citizenship with Missing Records: Building alternative evidence packages using parish records, military conscription lists, state archive duplicates, and American immigration documents when standard vital records are unavailable.
- Narrative Family History: Custom-written family history books and reports transforming genealogical records into readable family stories.

## Italian Citizenship Law (Current as of April 2026)
- Law 74/2025 (Tajani Decree): Limits jure sanguinis recognition to two generations — parent or grandparent born in Italy. Applications filed before March 27, 2025 follow previous rules.
- Constitutional Court ruling (March 12, 2026): Upheld the reform as constitutional.
- 1948 Cases: Judicial remedy for maternal-line discrimination remains available but subject to new generational limits.
- Centralization (Law 11/2026): Adult citizenship processing moves to a centralized Rome office from January 1, 2029.

## Service Areas
- United States: All 50 states, with particular client concentration in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, Rhode Island, Massachusetts, California, Illinois, Florida.
- Italy: Direct archive access across all 20 regions.
